key_points: Honors dorm waitlist is real — admitted students may not get in
Commuter school reputation means campus housing demand is lower
Limited student data on housing quality from available sources
key_quotes: I'm in the honors college (wait listed for honors dorms tho :/)

key_points: Heavily Mormon-influenced culture makes non-Mormon students feel excluded
LGBTQ+ students report feeling unsafe and unable to be themselves
Strong sports culture exists but overall social scene is niche
key_quotes: Utah is most definitely not the state to go if you're not straight and white
I feel like it's kinda dumb to go to Utah unless u are a white man or woman who intends on being a stay at home mom with 6 kids. Or if u are Mormon
it's like a whole different world out there. I can't imagine anyone saying most of these things with straight faces in any school in Boston

key_points: No specific dining data available from student posts or comments
Salt Lake City proximity gives off-campus food options
Campus dining not a frequent complaint or praise point

key_points: Multiple students report depression, isolation, and dropping out at UofU
One student went from California, became suicidal, and dropped out
Transition shock hits hard — especially for out-of-state students

key_points: Notably generous merit scholarships, especially for out-of-state students
Full rides available through programs like Business Scholars
Financial aid communication can lag — students wait weeks for packets
key_quotes: I'm withdrawing my app from UMich and going to University of Utah with a full ride in the Business Scholars program!
they are incredibly generous with scholarships, especially for out of state kids
University of Utah accepted me... they said they would already give me my financial aid info it's not anywhere on my account

key_points: CS and engineering programs are genuinely well-regarded nationally
Game design program ranks high on Princeton Review
No neuroscience undergrad major is a specific gap students have hit
key_quotes: University of Utah is awesome for Computer Science!
Utah is actually pretty well known for CS, and they have a great program
they don't offer a neuroscience major, which is what I was hoping to study

key_points: Trans student protester group punished while transphobic group was protected
Transfer credit and nursing waitlist processes cause multi-year delays
Financial aid packet delays leave accepted students in the dark
key_quotes: The group that protested was punished for protesting, while the other group was protected by the university

key_points: Salt Lake City and outdoor access (skiing, cycling, hiking) are major draws
Campus is viewed as a commuter school — can feel dead on weekends
45 minutes from Park City ski resorts is a genuine perk

apparently it is a commuter college
if you like doing things outside, definitely go to Utah

key_points: CS grads get recruited to Bay Area tech — both ASU and Utah feed Silicon Valley
Finance is weak — BYU is closer to Wall Street target status than UofU
Strong local Utah job connections if you plan to stay in-state

it has good connections in the area... if you want to live and work in Utah
